title: cytisus capitatus subsp. prostratus
intro: Cytisus capitatus subsp. prostratus, also known as the prostrate broom or shrubby broom, is a subspecies of the Cytisus capitatus plant, a type of flowering shrub in the legume family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of gastrointestinal issues, due to its anthraquinone content. The health benefits of Cytisus capitatus subsp. prostratus include its ability to stimulate bowel movements, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as infusions and decoctions, have been used to treat constipation, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal disorders.
